_note_totalYears: Average 17 years from discovery to first commercial production as of early 2020s. Was 13 years for projects commissioned in late 2000s — timeline is lengthening. Source: RESOURCES-summary.md.

stages.name: Discovery & exploration
stages.description: Geological survey, drilling, resource estimation, and feasibility study.

stages.name: Permitting & environmental review
stages.description: Regulatory filings, environmental impact assessment, community consultation. More than two-thirds of total timeline spent here and in legal challenges.

stages.name: Legal challenges & appeals
stages.description: Opposition litigation, appeals, Indigenous rights consultations. The single largest time sink in the modern mining cycle.
stages._note: Legal/permitting phases together consume >67% of the 17-year timeline.

stages.name: Construction
stages.description: Mine infrastructure build-out: access roads, processing plant, tailings facilities, power and water connections.

stages.name: First production
stages.description: Commercial ore production begins. Supply response to the 2024–2026 AI demand surge arrives no earlier than the late 2030s.

presets.label: 2024 AI Boom
presets.note: The surge in AI chip demand that began with large language models. Any mine started in response to 2024 demand cannot produce until ~2041.

presets.label: 2026 Robotics Demand
presets.note: Projected humanoid robotics demand spike driving neodymium and rare earth demand. Response supply arrives ~2043.

keyMinerals.use: Permanent magnets in robot actuators and EV motors
keyMinerals._note: China controls ~100% of dysprosium and dominates neodymium refining. Export controls introduced 2024–2025.

keyMinerals.use: Electrical wiring, data center busbars, EV motors
keyMinerals._note: China's share of global copper concentrate imports rose from 43% (2015) to 66% (2024). Source: RESOURCES-summary.md.
